structure.notes: The required Stage 0 sections, locked PartialID parameters, labelled assumptions, and actionable math-object checklist are present; the needed repair is in the setup/front-matter statement, not the skeleton.

novelty.notes: Positioning is targeted to continuous-treatment DiD, shape-restricted PartialID, LP-valued bounds, and in-repository predecessors, with a theorem-level distinction between algebraic envelopes, law-sharp completion, critical frontiers, and relaxed LP inference.

correctness.notes: D0.5.1 reproduced the corrected mean-completion law-sharp theorem but found that the front-matter law-sharp equality still states the uncorrected envelope and omits necessary mean-completion inequalities.

perItemFindings.label: Motivating Conjecture 1 / front-matter law-sharp equality
perItemFindings.one_line: The front-matter equality still claims law-sharpness of the original algebraic envelope [L_h(L;P),U_h(L;P)] even though bounded off-arm support requires the additional inequalities lower y <= beta_h(P)+alpha_jh <= upper y.

perItemFindings.label: Theorem 3 conditional law-sharp coverage cross-reference
perItemFindings.one_line: The proof invokes Conjecture law-sharp as if it yielded Theta_I(P)=[L_h(L;P),U_h(L;P)], but the reproduced corrected result yields the mean-completion interval [L_h^mc(L;P),U_h^mc(L;P)].

verbatim_critique: Revise the shared setup and front matter so the law-sharp object is consistently the mean-completion polytope. In the abstract and Introduction's Motivating Conjecture 1, replace the equality with [L_h(L;P),U_h(L;P)] by the corrected [L_h^mc(L;P),U_h^mc(L;P)] statement, or explicitly state that the original algebraic envelope is only an outer algebraic bound and law-sharpness holds after adding the mean-completion inequalities. The required inequalities are lower y <= beta_h(P)+alpha_jh <= upper y for every off-arm j != h, with beta_h(P)=mu_1h(P)-tau_h(P). Also revise the Theorem 3 proof's conditional law-sharp coverage paragraph: it should not assert Theta_I(P)=[L_h(L;P),U_h(L;P)] from the corrected law-sharp theorem. State instead that the corrected law-sharp interval is [L_h^mc(L;P),U_h^mc(L;P)] and that coverage by the relaxed original algebraic interval follows only through the containment [L_h^mc,U_h^mc] subset [L_h,U_h] subset [hat L_h,hat U_h], or update the sample LP to cover the corrected mean-completion endpoints directly.
